title: Software Engineer
description:
Job description
Software Engineer – Healthcare Information Systems / AI
📍 Location: San Gabriel (In Person ONlY)
🏥 Organization: Large Integrated Medical Center
🕒 Employment Type: Full-time
Position Overview
We are a large integrated healthcare system committed to improving clinical efficiency, quality of care, and patient safety through technology and innovation.
We are seeking a Software Engineer to join our medical informatics and AI initiatives, focused on building intelligent systems that transform unstructured clinical data into structured, actionable insights.
This role goes beyond traditional software development — it requires translating complex clinical documentation and workflows into AI-powered tools that support real-world medical decision-making and documentation quality.
You will work directly with physicians and clinical teams to design systems that improve how clinical information is captured, structured, and utilized.
Responsibilities
Design and build systems that convert unstructured clinical notes (e.g., ER notes, H&P) into structured, high-quality clinical documentation
Develop AI-powered features using LLMs/NLP for clinical text processing, summarization, and reasoning
Translate clinical workflows (e.g., inpatient admission logic, documentation requirements) into scalable software solutions
Integrate clinical data sources and guidelines into decision-support tools
Collaborate closely with physicians to iterate on real-world usability and accuracy
Ensure system reliability, performance, and compliance with healthcare standards
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field
Strong problem-solving ability and system design thinking
Experience building end-to-end features (not just isolated components)
Proficiency in at least one programming language (Python strongly preferred)
Experience working with APIs, backend systems, or full-stack applications
Ability to work with ambiguous, real-world data and translate it into structured outputs
Strong communication skills when working with non-technical stakeholders
Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have)
Experience with healthcare software, EHR/EMR systems, or clinical data platforms
Background in AI, machine learning, NLP, or data processing
Familiarity with healthcare privacy, security, or regulatory requirements
Experience building systems in highly regulated industries
What We Offer
Opportunity to build software used in real clinical environments
Close collaboration with frontline clinicians and healthcare experts
Stable, long-term role with meaningful social impact
Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits
A unique career path at the intersection of technology and clinical care
location: San Gabriel, CA, or Los Angeles, CA
